name: Palestine
experiences: Church of the Nativity and Bethlehem's Star Street
Banksy murals and The Walled Off Hotel museum
Ramallah café culture and museum stops
Jericho's Mount of Temptation cable car and Hisham's Palace
Hebron Old City lanes and the Ibrahimi Mosque
avoid: Jericho gets intensely hot from June to August, especially from late morning onward.

Safety advisory: checkpoint timings, protest-related closures, and access rules in the West Bank can change quickly, so recheck live government advisories, local hotel guidance, and same-day road conditions before every intercity leg.
Friday prayer hours, Sunday church traffic, and religious holidays can shorten opening hours around Bethlehem and Hebron, so start Old City visits early.
stopoverNote: A compact 4- to 5-day Bethlehem + Hebron + Ramallah + Jericho loop works well when checkpoints are calm; use 6 to 7 days only if you want slower café time in Ramallah or an extra Jericho desert day.

cities.name: Bethlehem
cities.notes: Manger Square base for the Church of the Nativity, Star Street, Banksy wall art, traditional guesthouses and easy walking between most core sights

cities.name: Ramallah
cities.notes: The West Bank's easiest urban base for cafés, cultural venues, central hotels and onward shared taxis to other cities

cities.name: Jericho
cities.notes: Low-lying oasis city for the Mount of Temptation, Hisham's Palace, archaeological sites and quick palm-lined road trips

cities.name: Hebron
cities.notes: Historic Al-Khalil for the Ibrahimi Mosque, old souq lanes, ceramic and glass workshops, and one of the West Bank's most layered old cities

itinerary.sim: Jawwal or Ooredoo Palestine prepaid SIMs usually land around ₹460–₹1,380 depending on data size, which is enough for offline maps, WhatsApp calls, and booking short taxi hops when shared service taxis are not practical.

Bethlehem.name: Bethlehem
Bethlehem.note: Stay near Manger Square or Beit Sahour so the Church of the Nativity, Star Street, the old market and the wall-art circuit are all walkable without depending on taxis.

days.theme: Cover Bethlehem's pilgrimage core first, then slow down for cafés and the evening atmosphere around the square

activities.name: Church of the Nativity and St. Catherine's Church
Manger Square and Star Street walk
Milk Grotto
Afteem Restaurant lunch stop

days.theme: Use the second day for Banksy-focused Bethlehem and a more contemporary read on the city beyond the church quarter

activities.name: Banksy murals along the separation wall
The Walled Off Hotel museum
International Nativity Museum
Old market and local sweets stop

Hebron.name: Hebron
Hebron.note: Hebron works best as a focused daytime visit with an early arrival; keep your passport handy, dress conservatively, and leave buffer time for checkpoints or security screening near the old city.

days.theme: Give Hebron a full old-city day for religious history, market lanes and working craft traditions

activities.name: Ibrahimi Mosque / Cave of the Patriarchs
Hebron Old City and souq walk
Hirbawi keffiyeh factory shop visit
Hebron Glass & Ceramics Factory

Ramallah.name: Ramallah
Ramallah.note: Ramallah is the easiest place to recover between religious-history days: most sights are short taxi hops apart, and central hotels make evening food and café crawls simple on foot.

days.theme: Switch to a more contemporary urban day with museums, public squares and Ramallah's strong café culture

activities.name: Al-Manara Square and central Ramallah walk
Yasser Arafat Museum and mausoleum grounds
Dar Zahran Heritage Building
Evening café hop around Rukab Street

days.theme: Add a second Ramallah day only if you want a slower culture day or a nearby village side trip rather than another big Old City

activities.name: The Palestinian Museum in Birzeit
Khalil Sakakini Cultural Center
Taybeh village outing for church lanes and local tastings

Jericho.name: Jericho
Jericho.note: Start early in Jericho because the valley heat rises fast; use walking only for the compact center and rely on short taxis or service rides for Mount of Temptation and Hisham's Palace.

days.theme: Do Jericho's headline sites in one efficient day by starting with the cable car before the worst of the heat

activities.name: Mount of Temptation cable car (round trip)
Monastery lookout above Jericho
Hisham's Palace
Jericho city center and Zacchaeus Tree stop

days.theme: Keep a second Jericho day only if you want a slower oasis pace, pool time or more archaeology

activities.name: Tell es-Sultan / Ancient Jericho
Ain es-Sultan spring area walk
Jericho resort pool or spa day-use

connections.method: Shared service taxi (servees) or local bus, then walking inside the old city
Shared service taxi via Bethlehem or direct service when available
Shared service taxi or bus from Ramallah/Al-Bireh transport hub

extras: Safety advisory: checkpoint wait times, protest-related road closures, and access restrictions can change with little notice, so confirm the latest local situation before departure and avoid demonstrations altogether.
Carry ILS cash for service taxis, museum tickets and small eateries because cards are still hit-or-miss outside larger hotels in Bethlehem and Ramallah.
Church of the Nativity and the Ibrahimi Mosque are free to enter, but modest dress and extra security screening time are both sensible.
The Walled Off Hotel museum is about 20 ILS (~₹460), Hisham's Palace about 10 ILS (~₹230), and the Mount of Temptation cable car about 60 ILS round trip (~₹1,380).
Walking works well in Manger Square, central Ramallah and parts of Hebron Old City, but Jericho is much easier with short taxi hops because distances and heat add up quickly.

Jericho: Mount of Temptation cable car Jericho
